001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.bean.swagger.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.converter.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.matcher.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.swagger.*;
035
036/**
037 * Identifies a REST PATH operation Java method on a {@link RestServlet} implementation class.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * This is a specialized subtype of <c><ja>{@link RestOp @RestOp}(method=<jsf>PATCH</jsf>)</c>.
041 *
042 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
043 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestOpAnnotatedMethods">@RestOp-Annotated Method Basics</a>
044
045 * </ul>
046 */
047@Target(METHOD)
048@Retention(RUNTIME)
049@Inherited
050@AnnotationGroup(RestOp.class)
051public @interface RestPatch {
052
053   /**
054    * Specifies whether this method can be called based on the client version.
055    *
056    * <p>
057    * The client version is identified via the HTTP request header identified by
058    * {@link Rest#clientVersionHeader() @Rest(clientVersionHeader)} which by default is <js>"Client-Version"</js>.
059    *
060    * <p>
061    * This is a specialized kind of {@link RestMatcher} that allows you to invoke different Java methods for the same
062    * method/path based on the client version.
063    *
064    * <p>
065    * The format of the client version range is similar to that of OSGi versions.
066    *
067    * <p>
068    * In the following example, the Java methods are mapped to the same HTTP method and URL <js>"/foobar"</js>.
069    * <p class='bjava'>
070    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.
071    *    // Note that this also matches 2.0.1.</jc>
072    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
073    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method1()  {...}
074    *
075    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
076    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
077    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method2()  {...}
078    *
079    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is less than 1.1.</jc>
080    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[0,1.1)"</js>)
081    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method3()  {...}
082    * </p>
083    *
084    * <p>
085    * It's common to combine the client version with transforms that will convert new POJOs into older POJOs for
086    * backwards compatibility.
087    * <p class='bjava'>
088    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.</jc>
089    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
090    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo newMethod()  {...}
091    *
092    *    <jc>// Call this method if X-Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
093    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
094    *    <ja>@MarshalledConfig</ja>(swaps=NewToOldSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
095    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo oldMethod() {
096    *       <jk>return</jk> newMethod();
097    *    }
098    *
099    * <p>
100    * Note that in the previous example, we're returning the exact same POJO, but using a transform to convert it into
101    * an older form.
102    * The old method could also just return back a completely different object.
103    * The range can be any of the following:
104    * <ul>
105    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0)"</js> = Less than 1.0.  1.0 and 1.0.0 does not match.
106    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0]"</js> = Less than or equal to 1.0.  Note that 1.0.1 will match.
107    *    <li><js>"1.0"</js> = At least 1.0.  1.0 and 2.0 will match.
108    * </ul>
109    *
110    *
111    * @return The annotation value.
112    */
113   String clientVersion() default "";
114
115   /**
116    * Supported content media types.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * Overrides the media types inferred from the parsers that identify what media types can be consumed by the resource.
120    *
121    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
122    *    <li class='note'>
123    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
124    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
125    * </ul>
126    *
127    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
128    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestOpContext#getSupportedContentTypes()}
129    * </ul>
130    *
131    * @return The annotation value.
132    */
133   String[] consumes() default {};
134
135   /**
136    * Class-level response converters.
137    *
138    * <p>
139    * Associates one or more {@link RestConverter converters} with this method.
140    *
141    *
142    * @return The annotation value.
143    */
144   Class<? extends RestConverter>[] converters() default {};
145
146   /**
147    * Enable debug mode.
148    *
149    * <p>
150    * Enables the following:
151    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
152    *    <li>
153    *       HTTP request/response bodies are cached in memory for logging purposes.
154    *    <li>
155    *       Request/response messages are automatically logged.
156    * </ul>
157    *
158    * <ul class='values'>
159    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Debug is enabled for all requests.
160    *    <li><js>"false"</js> - Debug is disabled for all requests.
161    *    <li><js>"conditional"</js> - Debug is enabled only for requests that have a <c class='snippet'>Debug: true</c> header.
162    *    <li><js>""</js> (or anything else) - Debug mode is inherited from class.
163    * </ul>
164    *
165    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
166    *    <li class='note'>
167    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
168    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
169    * </ul>
170    *
171    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
172    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestContext#getDebugEnablement()}
173    * </ul>
174    *
175    * @return The annotation value.
176    */
177   String debug() default "";
178
179   /**
180    * Default <c>Accept</c> header.
181    *
182    * <p>
183    * The default value for the <c>Accept</c> header if not specified on a request.
184    *
185    * <p>
186    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
187    *
188    * @return The annotation value.
189    */
190   String defaultAccept() default "";
191
192   /**
193    * Default character encoding.
194    *
195    * <p>
196    * The default character encoding for the request and response if not specified on the request.
197    *
198    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
199    *    <li class='note'>
200    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
201    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
202    * </ul>
203    *
204    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
205    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultCharset}
206    * </ul>
207    *
208    * @return The annotation value.
209    */
210   String defaultCharset() default "";
211
212   /**
213    * Default <c>Content-Type</c> header.
214    *
215    * <p>
216    * The default value for the <c>Content-Type</c> header if not specified on a request.
217    *
218    * <p>
219    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
220    *
221    * @return The annotation value.
222    */
223   String defaultContentType() default "";
224
225   /**
226    * Default request attributes.
227    *
228    * <p>
229    * Specifies default values for request attributes if they're not already set on the request.
230    *
231    * <p>
232    * Affects values returned by the following methods:
233    *    <ul>
234    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttribute(String)}.
235    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttributes()}.
236    *    </ul>
237    *
238    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
239    * <p class='bjava'>
240    *    <jc>// Defined via annotation resolving to a config file setting with default value.</jc>
241    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo=bar"</js>, <js>"Baz: $C{REST/myAttributeValue}"</js>})
242    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
243    *
244    *       <jc>// Override at the method level.</jc>
245    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo: bar"</js>})
246    *       <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod() {...}
247    *    }
248    * </p>
249    *
250    * </ul>
251    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
252    *    <li class='note'>
253    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
254    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
255    * </ul>
256    *
257    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>  *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultRequestAttributes()}
258    * </ul>
259    *
260    * @return The annotation value.
261    */
262   String[] defaultRequestAttributes() default {};
263
264   /**
265    * Specifies default values for form-data parameters.
266    *
267    * <p>
268    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
269    *
270    * <p>
271    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getFormParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the
272    * request.
273    *
274    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
275    * <p class='bjava'>
276    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestFormData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
277    *    <jk>public</jk> String doGet(<ja>@FormData</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
278    * </p>
279    *
280    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
281    *    <li class='note'>
282    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
283    *    <li class='note'>
284    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
285    *    <li class='note'>
286    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
287    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
288    * </ul>
289    *
290    * @return The annotation value.
291    */
292   String[] defaultRequestFormData() default {};
293
294   /**
295    * Default request headers.
296    *
297    * <p>
298    * Specifies default values for request headers if they're not passed in through the request.
299    *
300    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
301    * <p class='bjava'>
302    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
303    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestHeaders={<js>"Accept: text/json"</js>})
304    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPost()  {...}
305    * </p>
306    *
307    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
308    *    <li class='note'>
309    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
310    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
311    * </ul>
312    *
313    *
314    * @return The annotation value.
315    */
316   String[] defaultRequestHeaders() default {};
317
318   /**
319    * Specifies default values for query parameters.
320    *
321    * <p>
322    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
323    *
324    * <p>
325    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getQueryParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the request.
326    *
327    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
328    * <p class='bjava'>
329    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestQueryData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
330    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPost(<ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
331    * </p>
332    *
333    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
334    *    <li class='note'>
335    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
336    *    <li class='note'>
337    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
338    *    <li class='note'>
339    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
340    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
341    * </ul>
342    *
343    * @return The annotation value.
344    */
345   String[] defaultRequestQueryData() default {};
346
347   /**
348    * Default response headers.
349    *
350    * <p>
351    * Specifies default values for response headers if they're not overwritten during the request.
352    *
353    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
354    * <p class='bjava'>
355    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
356    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultResponseHeaders={<js>"Content-Type: text/json"</js>})
357    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPost()  {...}
358    * </p>
359    *
360    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
361    *    <li class='note'>
362    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
363    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
364    * </ul>
365    *
366    *
367    * @return The annotation value.
368    */
369   String[] defaultResponseHeaders() default {};
370
371   /**
372    * Optional description for the exposed API.
373    *
374    * <p>
375    * This description is used in the following locations:
376    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
377    *    <li>
378    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getDescription()} in the auto-generated swagger.
379    *    <li>
380    *       The <js>"$RS{operationDescription}"</js> variable.
381    *    <li>
382    *       The description of the method in the Swagger page.
383    * </ul>
384    *
385    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
386    *    <li class='note'>
387    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/description</c>.
388    *    <li class='note'>
389    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
390    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
391    * </ul>
392    *
393    * @return The annotation value.
394    */
395   String[] description() default {};
396
397   /**
398    * Specifies the compression encoders for this method.
399    *
400    * <p>
401    * Encoders are used to enable various kinds of compression (e.g. <js>"gzip"</js>) on requests and responses.
402    *
403    * <p>
404    * This value overrides encoders specified at the class level using {@link Rest#encoders()}.
405    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
406    *
407    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
408    * <p class='bjava'>
409    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that handles GZIP compression.</jc>
410    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
411    *       encoders={
412    *          GzipEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
413    *       }
414    *    )
415    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
416    *
417    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also use a custom encoder.</jc>
418    *       <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
419    *          encoders={
420    *             EncoderSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, MyEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
421    *          }
422    *       )
423    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPost(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
424    *          ...
425    *       }
426    *    }
427    * </p>
428    *
429    * <p>
430    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link EncoderSet} bean via
431    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="encoders")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
432    *
433    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
434    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerEncoders">Encoders</a>
435    * </ul>
436    *
437    * @return The annotation value.
438    */
439   Class<? extends Encoder>[] encoders() default {};
440
441   /**
442    * Method-level guards.
443    *
444    * <p>
445    * Associates one or more {@link RestGuard RestGuards} with this method.
446    *
447    *
448    * @return The annotation value.
449    */
450   Class<? extends RestGuard>[] guards() default {};
451
452   /**
453    * Method matchers.
454    *
455    * <p>
456    * Associates one more more {@link RestMatcher RestMatchers} with this method.
457    *
458    * <p>
459    * Matchers are used to allow multiple Java methods to handle requests assigned to the same URL path pattern, but
460    * differing based on some request attribute, such as a specific header value.
461    *
462    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
463    *    <li class='jac'>{@link RestMatcher}
464    * </ul>
465    *
466    * @return The annotation value.
467    */
468   Class<? extends RestMatcher>[] matchers() default {};
469
470   /**
471    * The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.
472    *
473    * <p>
474    * Useful for alleviating DoS attacks by throwing an exception when too much input is received instead of resulting
475    * in out-of-memory errors which could affect system stability.
476    *
477    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
478    * <p class='bjava'>
479    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
480    *       maxInput=<js>"100M"</js>
481    *    )
482    * </p>
483    *
484    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
485    *    <li class='note'>
486    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
487    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
488    * </ul>
489    *
490    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
491    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#maxInput}
492    * </ul>
493    *
494    * @return The annotation value.
495    */
496   String maxInput() default "";
497
498   /**
499    * Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs for this method.
500    *
501    * <p>
502    * Parsers are used to convert the content of HTTP requests into POJOs.
503    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework parsers can be used in this setting.
504    * <br>The parser selected is based on the request <c>Content-Type</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
505    * using a best-match algorithm:
506    * <ul class='javatree'>
507    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Parser#getMediaTypes()}
508    * </ul>
509    *
510    * <p>
511    * This value overrides parsers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#parsers()}.
512    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
513    *
514    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
515    * <p class='bjava'>
516    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can consume JSON and HTML.</jc>
517    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
518    *       parsers={
519    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
520    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
521    *       }
522    *    )
523    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
524    *
525    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also consume XML.</jc>
526    *       <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
527    *          parsers={
528    *             ParserSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
529    *          }
530    *       )
531    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPost(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
532    *          ...
533    *       }
534    *    }
535    * </p>
536    *
537    * <p>
538    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link ParserSet} bean via
539    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="parsers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
540    *
541    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
542    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
543    * </ul>
544    *
545    * @return The annotation value.
546    */
547   Class<?>[] parsers() default {};
548
549   /**
550    * Optional path pattern for the specified method.
551    *
552    * <p>
553    * Appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the path pattern will make it match any remainder too.
554    * <br>Not appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the pattern will cause a 404 (Not found) error to occur if the exact
555    * pattern is not found.
556    *
557    * <p>
558    * The path can contain variables that get resolved to {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path @Path} parameters.
559    *
560    * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
561    * <p class='bjava'>
562    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/myurl/{foo}/{bar}/{baz}/*"</js>)
563    * </p>
564    * <p class='bjava'>
565    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/myurl/{0}/{1}/{2}/*"</js>)
566    * </p>
567    *
568    * <p>
569    * If you do not specify a path name, then the path name is inferred from the Java method name.
570    *
571    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
572    * <p class='bjava'>
573    *    <jc>// Path is assumed to be "/foo".</jc>
574    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>
575    *    <jk>public void</jk> foo() {...}
576    * </p>
577    *
578    * <p>
579    * Note that you can also use {@link #value()} to specify the path.
580    *
581    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
582    *    <li class='ja'>{@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path}
583    * </ul>
584    *
585    * @return The annotation value.
586    */
587   String[] path() default {};
588
589   /**
590    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#problemDetails() @Rest(problemDetails)}.
591    *
592    * <p>
593    * When set on a {@code @RestPatch} method, this value takes precedence over the resource-level
594    * {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} attribute for this operation. Tri-state semantics:
595    *
596    * <ul class='values'>
597    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable problem-details responses for this operation.
598    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; disable problem-details responses for this operation (overrides an
599    *       opted-in resource).
600    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from {@link Rest#problemDetails()}.
601    * </ul>
602    *
603    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
604    *    <li class='note'>
605    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
606    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
607    * </ul>
608    *
609    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
610    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#problemDetails()}
611    * </ul>
612    *
613    * @return The annotation value.
614    */
615   String problemDetails() default "";
616
617   /**
618    * Per-operation observability opt-in / opt-out control.
619    *
620    * <p>
621    * Overrides the resource-level {@link Rest#observability()} setting for this specific operation.
622    *
623    * <ul class='values'>
624    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; strict opt-in; requires a wired observability backend.
625    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; explicit opt-out; short-circuits the observability block.
626    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherits the resource-level setting.
627    * </ul>
628    *
629    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
630    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#observability()}
631    * </ul>
632    *
633    * @return The annotation value.
634    * @since 10.0.0
635    */
636   String observability() default "";
637
638   /**
639    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor() @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)}.
640    *
641    * <p>
642    * Names the {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} bean used to route completion callbacks for this
643    * specific operation through a dedicated thread pool. Empty string (default) inherits from
644    * {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}.
645    *
646    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
647    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
648    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
649    * </ul>
650    *
651    * @return The annotation value.
652    * @since 10.0.0
653    */
654   String asyncCompletionExecutor() default "";
655
656   /**
657    * Per-operation metric name override. Empty string (default) uses the recorder's own name derivation.
658    *
659    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
660    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#metricName()}
661    * </ul>
662    *
663    * @return The annotation value.
664    * @since 10.0.0
665    */
666   String metricName() default "";
667
668   /**
669    * Per-operation metric tags. Format: comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs.
670    * Empty string (default) means no additional tags.
671    *
672    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
673    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#metricTags()}
674    * </ul>
675    *
676    * @return The annotation value.
677    * @since 10.0.0
678    */
679   String metricTags() default "";
680
681   /**
682    * Supported accept media types.
683    *
684    * <p>
685    * Overrides the media types inferred from the serializers that identify what media types can be produced by the resource.
686    *
687    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
688    *    <li class='note'>
689    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
690    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
691    * </ul>
692    *
693    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
694    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestOpContext#getSupportedAcceptTypes()}
695    * </ul>
696    *
697    * @return The annotation value.
698    */
699   String[] produces() default {};
700
701   /**
702    * Allowed serializer session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
703    *
704    * <p>
705    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Serializer-Options"</js>
706    * header or <js>"juneauSerializerOptions"</js> query parameter.
707    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedSerializerOptions()} values.
708    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
709    *
710    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
711    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
712    * </ul>
713    *
714    * @return The annotation value.
715    */
716   String[] allowedSerializerOptions() default {};
717
718   /**
719    * Allowed parser session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
720    *
721    * <p>
722    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Parser-Options"</js>
723    * header or <js>"juneauParserOptions"</js> query parameter.
724    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedParserOptions()} values.
725    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
726    *
727    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
728    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
729    * </ul>
730    *
731    * @return The annotation value.
732    */
733   String[] allowedParserOptions() default {};
734
735   /**
736    * Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.
737    *
738    * <p>
739    * Accepted values: {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"}, {@code "allowedParserOptions"}.
740    * Prevents the named property from inheriting values from the enclosing {@code @Rest} annotation.
741    * The {@code noInherit} attribute itself is never inherited.
742    *
743    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
744    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions">Session Options</a>
745    * </ul>
746    *
747    * @return The annotation value.
748    */
749   String[] noInherit() default {};
750
751   /**
752    * Role guard.
753    *
754    * <p>
755    * An expression defining if a user with the specified roles are allowed to access this method.
756    *
757    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
758    * <p class='bjava'>
759    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
760    *
761    *       <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
762    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
763    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
764    *       )
765    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doPost() {
766    *       }
767    *    }
768    * </p>
769    *
770    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
771    *    <li class='note'>
772    *       Supports any of the following expression constructs:
773    *       <ul>
774    *          <li><js>"foo"</js> - Single arguments.
775    *          <li><js>"foo,bar,baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments.
776    *          <li><js>"foo | bar | baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, pipe syntax.
777    *          <li><js>"foo || bar || baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, Java-OR syntax.
778    *          <li><js>"fo*"</js> - Patterns including <js>'*'</js> and <js>'?'</js>.
779    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, ampersand syntax.
780    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp;&amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, Java-AND syntax.
781    *          <li><js>"fo* || (*oo || bar)"</js> - Parenthesis.
782    *       </ul>
783    *    <li class='note'>
784    *       AND operations take precedence over OR operations (as expected).
785    *    <li class='note'>
786    *       Whitespace is ignored.
787    *    <li class='note'>
788    *       <jk>null</jk> or empty expressions always match as <jk>false</jk>.
789    *    <li class='note'>
790    *       If patterns are used, you must specify the list of declared roles using {@link #rolesDeclared()}.
791    *    <li class='note'>
792    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
793    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
794    *    <li class='note'>
795    *       When defined on parent/child classes and methods, ALL guards within the hierarchy must pass.
796    * </ul>
797    *
798    *
799    * @return The annotation value.
800    */
801   String roleGuard() default "";
802
803   /**
804    * Declared roles.
805    *
806    * <p>
807    * A comma-delimited list of all possible user roles.
808    *
809    * <p>
810    * Used in conjunction with {@link #roleGuard()} is used with patterns.
811    *
812    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
813    * <p class='bjava'>
814    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
815    *
816    *       <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
817    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
818    *          rolesDeclared=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN,ROLE_READ_WRITE,ROLE_READ_ONLY,ROLE_SPECIAL"</js>,
819    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
820    *       )
821    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doPost() {
822    *       }
823    *    }
824    * </p>
825    *
826    *
827    * @return The annotation value.
828    */
829   String rolesDeclared() default "";
830
831   /**
832    * Specifies the serializers for marshalling POJOs into response bodies for this method.
833    *
834    * <p>
835    * Serializer are used to convert POJOs to HTTP response bodies.
836    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework serializers can be used in this setting.
837    * <br>The serializer selected is based on the request <c>Accept</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
838    * using a best-match algorithm:
839    * <ul class='javatree'>
840    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Serializer#getMediaTypeRanges()}
841    * </ul>
842    *
843    * <p>
844    * This value overrides serializers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#serializers()}.
845    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
846    *
847    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
848    * <p class='bjava'>
849    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can produce JSON and HTML.</jc>
850    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
851    *       serializers={
852    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
853    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
854    *       }
855    *    )
856    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
857    *
858    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also produce XML.</jc>
859    *       <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
860    *          parsers={
861    *             SerializerSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
862    *          }
863    *       )
864    *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean doPost() {
865    *          ...
866    *       }
867    *    }
868    * </p>
869    *
870    * <p>
871    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link SerializerSet} bean via
872    * {@link Bean @Bean(name="serializers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
873    *
874    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
875    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
876    * </ul>
877    *
878    * @return The annotation value.
879    */
880   Class<? extends Serializer>[] serializers() default {};
881
882   /**
883    * Optional summary for the exposed API.
884    *
885    * <p>
886    * This summary is used in the following locations:
887    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
888    *    <li>
889    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getSummary()} in the auto-generated swagger.
890    *    <li>
891    *       The <js>"$RS{operationSummary}"</js> variable.
892    *    <li>
893    *       The summary of the method in the Swagger page.
894    * </ul>
895    *
896    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
897    *    <li class='note'>
898    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/summary</c>.
899    *    <li class='note'>
900    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
901    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
902    * </ul>
903    *
904    * @return The annotation value.
905    */
906   String summary() default "";
907
908   /**
909    * Provides swagger-specific metadata on this method.
910    *
911    * <p>
912    * Used to populate the auto-generated OPTIONS swagger documentation.
913    *
914    * <p>
915    * The format of this annotation is JSON when all individual parts are concatenated.
916    * <br>The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
917    *
918    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
919    * <p class='bjava'>
920    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(
921    *       path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>,
922    *
923    *       <jc>// Swagger info.</jc>
924    *       swagger={
925    *          <js>"parameters:["</js>,
926    *             <js>"{name:'propertyName',in:'path',description:'The system property name.'},"</js>,
927    *             <js>"{in:'body',description:'The new system property value.'}"</js>,
928    *          <js>"],"</js>,
929    *          <js>"responses:{"</js>,
930    *             <js>"302: {headers:{Location:{description:'The root URL of this resource.'}}},"</js>,
931    *             <js>"403: {description:'User is not an admin.'}"</js>,
932    *          <js>"}"</js>
933    *       }
934    *    )
935    * </p>
936    *
937    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
938    *    <li class='note'>
939    *       The format is <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauBeanSwagger2">juneau-bean-swagger-v2</a>.
940    *       <br>Multiple lines are concatenated with newlines.
941    *    <li class='note'>
942    *       The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
943    *    <li class='note'>
944    *       These values are superimposed on top of any Swagger JSON file present for the resource in the classpath.
945    *    <li class='note'>
946    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
947    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
948    * </ul>
949    *
950    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
951    *    <li class='ja'>{@link OpSwagger}
952    *    <li class='jc'>{@link SwaggerProvider}
953    * </ul>
954    *
955    * @return The annotation value.
956    */
957   OpSwagger swagger() default @OpSwagger;
958
959   /**
960    * REST method path.
961    *
962    * <p>
963    * Can be used to provide a shortened form for the {@link #path()} value.
964    *
965    * <p>
966    * The following examples are considered equivalent.
967    * <p class='bjava'>
968    *    <jc>// Normal form</jc>
969    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>)
970    *
971    *    <jc>// Shortened form</jc>
972    *    <ja>@RestPatch</ja>(<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>)
973    * </p>
974    *
975    * @return The annotation value.
976    */
977   String value() default "";
978}
